spec_match.rationale: The list target now generates and applies the actual list-style-image: image(<color>) declaration, with list-style-type retained only as the marker fallback.

interactive.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP testing exercised the property dropdown, tab buttons, text color input, quick palette, size slider, and Copy button with live preview and generated CSS updates.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The parent and concept blurbs promise background-image, list-style-image, border-image, and content, and the studio now uses those real target properties.

edge_cases.rationale: Unsupported Chrome 150 image(<color>) parsing is surfaced as an explicit fallback note, and invalid color input is rejected before it reaches the dynamic stylesheet.

references.rationale: The page includes the required ChromeStatus reference link.

design_system.rationale: The touched controls use local classes, design-system variables, accessible swatch buttons, and visible fallback states.

summary: Verified in Chrome 148 via chrome-devtools-mcp at /v150/css-image-color-function/authoring-studio/: the repaired list-style-image path generates list-style-image: image(#3b82f6), keeps list-style-type as the fallback marker, uses list items rather than a pseudo-element, and no longer emits a background-image marker snippet. Tested rgb(), oklch(), quick palette swatches, size changes, background, list, border, content tabs, invalid color input fallback, and Copy. The feature conformance route ran cleanly and showed 1 pass / 5 fail in Chrome 148, reflecting that this browser does not yet parse the Chrome 150 image(<color>) declarations.
